headline: Large Energy User Cost Shifting
short_summary: This bill shifts the cost of grid infrastructure from residential users to large energy users, primarily data centers, to reduce residential electricity bills and extends a power line burial program.
status: Last Action: Signed by President on 2026-04-22
supporters: Residential ratepayers, consumer advocacy groups, Senator Lucas, Delegate Bolling, Dominion Energy (with amendments).
opponents: Data center industry, large industrial users (initially, some concerns about governor's amendments).
news_headline: Governor amends bills that shift costs onto data centers.
news_summary: Governor Abigail Spanberger signed and suggested changes to HB 1393, a key energy measure designed to shift more costs onto data centers to save residential electricity customers money and expand a power line burial program. Critics argue her tweaks weaken the original intent, but supporters claim they will still save residential customers money by restricting Dominion's spending on line undergrounding and capping its return on equity.

headline: Data Center Water Use Reporting
short_summary: This bill requires utilities to report monthly water volumes supplied to data centers to the State Water Control Board, increasing transparency on water consumption.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups, Potomac Conservancy, local communities, water conservationists.
opponents: Data center industry (due to reporting burden/potential scrutiny).
news_headline: Potomac River Named Most Endangered in U.S. for 2026.
news_summary: The Potomac River was named the most endangered river in the U.S. for 2026, largely due to uncontrolled data center expansion and water demands. SB 553, which requires utilities to report monthly water volumes supplied to data centers, is seen as an important first step for transparency, though critics note it lacks individual facility disclosure and peak usage reporting.

headline: Data Center Siting & Noise Limits
short_summary: This bill allows localities to require site assessments for new high-energy-use facilities, including noise studies and impact reviews on natural resources, before zoning approval.
status: Last Action: Incorporated by Counties, Cities and Towns (HB153-Thomas) (Voice Vote) on 2026-01-23
supporters: Local communities, residents near proposed data center sites, Delegate Thomas.
opponents: Data center developers, real estate industry.
news_headline: Amid 'constant' data center noise, Sterling residents also worry about health impact.
news_summary: Residents in Sterling, Virginia, are experiencing constant noise from a nearby data center, leading to health concerns and calls for stricter noise ordinances. HB 153 aims to address such issues by requiring site assessments and noise studies for new data centers, giving localities more control over their placement and environmental impact.
